meaning: v.反抗,起义,反叛,反感,厌恶
examples: In February 1848, the people of Paris rose in revolt against the constitutional monarchy of Louis-Philippe.
If instead we regard the financing of warfare by taxation in this period as a background condition, then we will see the English reverse themselves as the main cause of the revolt.
And in the age of social media it was entirely predictable that customers would revolt vociferously.
Senate Republicans are on the verge of a full-scale revolt against the White House.
